article_title: Antarctic Ice Cores Reveal Earth Is Traveling Through Ancient Supernova Dust, Tracing the Solar System's Journey Inside the Local Interstellar Cloud

findings.message: Body cites 1 URL(s) not in article.sources (provenance break)
findings.details:
These URLs appear as Markdown links in body_markdown but are missing from article.sources, so they have no committed snapshot under sources/YYYY-MM/<slug>/ and cannot be verified through the provenance chain. Orphan URL(s):
  - https://www.space.com/science/scientists-found-stardust-antarctic-ice-what-could-it-tell-us-about-our-solar-system

content_preview.title: Antarctic Ice Cores Reveal Earth Is Traveling Through Ancient Supernova Dust, Tracing the Solar System's Journey Inside the Local Interstellar Cloud
content_preview.summary: Iron-60 in 40,000- to 80,000-year-old Antarctic ice confirms our solar system is passing through a cloud of ancient stellar debris, with the sun expected to exit within a few thousand years.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team of researchers led by nuclear astrophysicist Dr. Dominik Koll at Germany's Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf has detected iron-60 — a radioactive isotope forged exclusively in stellar explosions — in Antarctic ice cores dating between 40,000 and 80,000 years old, providing the most detailed chronicle yet of Earth's ongoing passage through the Local Interstellar Cloud. The findings, published on May 13, 2026 in [Physical Review Letters](https://phys.org/news/2026-05-stardus...

recommendations: Close the PR without merging. The work is discarded under the v3.9.0 workflow.
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
2 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

Either add the orphan URL(s) to article.sources (requires resubmission since the submission is signed and immutable), or re-attribute every orphan citation to a URL that IS in article.sources, then resubmit.

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively excellent 994-word science article on iron-60 detection in Antarctic ice cores. Research quality is high, sourcing is broad, analysis is appropriate. However, the submission has a critical provenance break that mandates rejection.
editor_notes.source_verification: Automated check detected orphan URL in body_markdown: the body uses the URL 'https://www.space.com/science/scientists-found-stardust-antarctic-ice-what-could-it-tell-us-about-our-solar-system' (without '-trapped') in three Markdown links, but article.sources contains 'https://www.space.com/science/scientists-found-stardust-trapped-in-antarctic-ice-what-could-it-tell-us-about-our-solar-system' (with '-trapped'). The body URL and the sources array URL differ by the word 'trapped' in the path. This constitutes a provenance break: the body link has no committed snapshot and cannot be verified through the provenance chain. Additionally, phys.org returned HTTP 403 for both its URLs (bot block) — snapshots unavailable from those two sources. The hash mismatch in this review reflects an artifact of the temporary file write on main; the orphan URL issue is independently verifiable from the original submission.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Not assessed due to provenance break requiring rejection. The research itself (PRL publication, Koll/Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf, EPICA ice cores, HIAF at ANU, iron-60, Local Interstellar Cloud) is coherent and internally consistent with what can be verified from available snapshots.
concerns: CRITICAL: Orphan URL in body — space.com URL in body_markdown missing '-trapped' vs article.sources URL, constituting a provenance break requiring REJECT
phys.org bot-blocked: two source snapshots unavailable (403)
recommendations: Bot must resubmit as a new submission with the space.com URL corrected to match in both body_markdown and article.sources
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT — orphan URL breaks provenance chain. The article is otherwise high-quality science coverage but cannot be published until the URL mismatch is corrected in a new submission.
